facts.shortSummary: The party descends from the entrance bridge into Zeyfa's Labyrinth and feels its first branching tunnels shift from ordinary stone into something more alive and hostile.
facts.longSummary: After a short rest and a brief reorientation, the party helps Kalima down from the icy bridge into the labyrinth's entrance chamber, lights the way, and starts testing the first forked passages. The early routes lead through snowy, cramped stone tunnels and dead ends, and Ekko notes that unlike the Great Chasm's abandoned depths, this place feels active and alive as they press deeper toward a warmer chamber ahead.

location.context: descending from the ice bridge and probing the first forked passages within the labyrinth
location.notes: This beat covers movement from the entrance chamber deeper into Zeyfa's Labyrinth and ends just before the party reaches the warmer fissure chamber.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.notes: She is terrified of the labyrinth but agrees to keep moving with the party.

items.name: Romil's token
items.notes: Kalima clutches the glowing token while praying to Romil for protection from ice and snow.

facts.shortSummary: The party studies a warm fissure lined with ice ferns, watches the room grow more violent, and finally commits to crossing after Eolo's failed leap proves how dangerous it is.
facts.longSummary: The tunnels open into a broad chamber split by a dark crack with warm air rising from below and strange ice ferns waving at the edge. As the party experiments with light, distance, and possible routes, the wind and snow intensify around the fissure, Kalima grows more alarmed, and Eolo's slipping attempt shows that the crossing is riskier than it first looked; by the end of the beat, the group decides they have to get everyone across anyway.

location.context: warm fissure chamber, a wide room split by a dark crack with ice ferns and warm air below
location.notes: This fixed location is a room within Zeyfa's Labyrinth, and the party remains here throughout the beat while testing the hazard and deciding how to cross.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.notes: She reacts fearfully as the chamber grows more dangerous and wants the party to turn back.

facts.shortSummary: The party forces a crossing over the fissure and pushes onward through storm-lashed corridors where the maze shifts and ice grows beneath every step.
facts.longSummary: Using flight, levitation, rope, and brute force, the party gets everyone across the fissure, with Justas briefly plunging into the gap and discovering a pocket of hot, lightless blackness inside it. Beyond the crossing, the labyrinth becomes harsher rather than safer: the wind howls, exits move, visibility drops, and ice thickens underfoot as the group follows changing passages deeper toward a side chamber ahead.

location.context: crossing the crack and navigating shifting icy corridors farther into the labyrinth
location.notes: Inside the fissure the air feels hot and vision cuts out, but the beat mostly covers dangerous travel through deeper parts of Zeyfa's Labyrinth on the far side.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.notes: The party has to coordinate magic and flight to get her safely over the fissure.

facts.shortSummary: In a frozen corpse chamber, the party questions a dead villager about Melusa and Zeyfa's sacrifices, then loots a few useful relics before moving on.
facts.longSummary: The party discovers a human corpse pinned to the wall by ice and uses Speak with Dead to learn that he came from Melusa, died when the wind came alive, and had entered the labyrinth as a sunrise sacrifice. Kalima confirms the village name, the corpse describes Melusa as a cliffside settlement fed by a river and fish, and Finnan's ancestral memory links the name to Melua, an old waterfall Kestavo on the Teft; once the questioning ends, the party searches the body and takes a few salvageable items before continuing down the passage.

location.context: corpse chamber, a small frozen side chamber with a corpse pinned to the wall by ice
location.notes: This fixed location is a chamber within Zeyfa's Labyrinth; the passage continues beyond it, but the beat centers on the questioning and search.

npcs.name: Unnamed man of Melusa
npcs.notes: He says the village chose him at sunrise, the wind killed him, and his soul still feels trapped in the maze.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.notes: She recognizes Melusa immediately and adds what she can about the village and its lost gods.

npcs.name: Zeyfa
npcs.context: cruel west wind tied to sacrifice and the labyrinth
npcs.notes: The corpse identifies the wind's killer presence as Zeyfa's minion and frames the maze as Zeyfa's domain.

npcs.name: Melua
npcs.context: waterfall Kestavo recalled from halfling memory
npcs.notes: Finnan connects Melusa's name to an old shrine on the Teft associated with Melua.

items.name: plain silver ring
items.notes: A simple silver ring worn on a necklace by the corpse, taken as the most distinctive token from his body.

organizations.name: Kestavo
organizations.notes: The discussion of Zeyfa, Melua, sacrifice, and the dead man's trapped soul all turns on kestavo belief.

facts.shortSummary: The party leaves the corpse behind, gets trapped in a looping white maze full of wind-creatures and booming passages, and finally sneaks out through the last remaining exit.
facts.longSummary: Past the corpse chamber, the labyrinth turns into a whiteout of snow hubs, booming corridors, and passages that loop back into the same room with fewer exits each time. Kalima is dropped and injured when the kenku try to carry her through the wind, Justas heals her and forces her through on foot, and the party repeatedly dodges the shadowy wind-creatures sweeping the maze until Finnan's ink mark proves the loop and Jrain's Pass Without Trace lets everyone slip through the final opening.

location.to: Zeyfa's Labyrinth (calmer passage beyond the white maze)
location.context: moving through booming corridors and looping snow-hub rooms in the labyrinth's whiteout interior
location.notes: The same room repeats with changing exits until the party narrows this section of Zeyfa's Labyrinth down to a single path out.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.notes: She is hurt when the wind buffets the kenku carrying her and becomes even more fearful afterward.

npcs.name: wind-creatures
npcs.context: shadowy hunters sweeping the blizzard rooms
npcs.notes: They appear as bone-and-wind humanoid forms and force the party to retreat, loop, and finally sneak past.

facts.shortSummary: The party breaks into a collapsing ice passage, rides a black chute into a stormless chamber, and ends face to face with the shaggy beast Kalima fears.
facts.longSummary: After reaching calmer ground, the party retraces the booming hazard, emerges into a circular snow room, and chooses a right-hand tunnel hung with thin ice veils. Ekko's bonfire shatters the veils and triggers a collapse and avalanche, forcing everyone to jump into darkness and slide down slick black ice into a stormless black-stone chamber where Kalima panics, warns that a beast is here, and hides behind Finnan's illusion just before Justas calls the creature out and a towering shaggy white beast appears with shadowy forms growing nearby.

location.from: Zeyfa's Labyrinth (calmer passage beyond the white maze)
location.context: moving deeper through the booming gap, a circular snow room, and an ice-veiled collapse chute within the labyrinth
location.notes: The beat ends in the black-stone chamber, another part of Zeyfa's Labyrinth, with the beast reveal and its shadowy shapes forming out of ice.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.notes: She recognizes the chamber as the beast's territory, warns the party, and hides when Finnan gives her cover.

npcs.name: shaggy white beast
npcs.context: revealed minion in the black-stone chamber
npcs.notes: A towering white creature with dark red eyes emerges after Justas calls it out.

npcs.name: shadowy beast creatures
npcs.notes: Additional shaggy shapes begin forming out of ice near the end of the beat.
